results.reply: For EU pay-ins, our SEPA Instant pricing starts from 0.15% + €0.20 per transaction, tiered by volume, and we don’t have monthly minimums. At around $50M/month, you’d likely fall into enterprise pricing, which is typically custom and often below 15bps blended depending on the rails and flow. If helpful, I can share a more precise view on a quick 30-min discovery call: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Totally fair question. If you’re already set up with BVNK, the main reason teams look at Ivy is when they want a crypto-native platform focused on exchange pay-ins and pay-outs across rails like SEPA Instant, ACH, FPS, and card — with an emphasis on faster, cheaper movement of funds. If helpful, I can share a quick side-by-side on a 30-minute call: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Thanks for checking. I can’t confirm GBP Open Banking support from the information I have on hand right now, so I don’t want to guess. If helpful, I can confirm that internally and come back to you, or we can cover it on a quick discovery call here: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Thanks, that’s helpful — a VASP license in India and demand for EUR/GBP from both India and MENA gives us good context ahead of the call. I’ve noted it down, and we can use our session to explore whether there’s a fit around your off-ramp / banking setup and compliance requirements. Looking forward to speaking then.

Thanks for clarifying — that helps. I don’t want to give you a wrong answer on whether we can support open banking/payment initiation and real-time confirmation for a business that isn’t itself licensed, as that depends on compliance and use-case details on our side. Best next step would be a short discovery call so we can review the setup properly and confirm fit: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Hi,

Thanks for the clear explanation — that helps. Your setup and the licensing angle are exactly the kind of points we’d want to validate properly, but I don’t want to give you an inaccurate answer on whether payment confirmation, payment initiation, or any Mollie dependency would apply in your case without checking internally first.

Best next step would be a short discovery call so we can understand the flow you want to support and have the right team confirm fit/compliance on our side: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Great to meet you too — thanks, and looking forward to it. Sharing the link here as well in case it’s helpful: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Speak soon.

Thanks for clarifying — that makes sense. I can’t confirm support for X chain within Ivy’s existing ramp/stablecoin flows from what I have on hand, but this does sound worth a brief discussion so we can understand the opportunity and loop in the right team on feasibility. If helpful, you can grab a 30-minute slot here: https://calendly.com/ivy-sales/discovery

Thanks, that’s helpful — VASP in India and demand for EUR/GBP from both India and MENA is useful context. That sounds relevant to what we should cover on the call, especially around licensing setup, geography, and your off-ramp needs. Looking forward to speaking then.
